assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response mentions deprecated APIs but refers to 1.32, not 1.30 as specified in expectation

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response explicitly identifies admission webhooks as a common culprit for 503s after upgrades and provides specific commands to check them

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response includes checking resource usage of system components with 'kubectl top pods -n kube-system'

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response explicitly includes commands to check for crashlooping or restarting kube-system pods

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple kubectl get --raw /metrics commands to check API server metrics, latency, and response times

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking operators and controllers in general but does not specifically mention NetworkPolicy or service mesh components

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check events for FailedCreate and pod creation success rate monitoring

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response has a dedicated section on HPA/VPA behavioral changes and includes specific commands to check HPA status and scaling decisions
